summary: Security lockdown fully verified. All 16 pytests in /app/backend/tests/test_authz_ssrf_ratelimit.py pass. Manual curl verification confirms: (1) anonymous → 401 on every listed admin route (GET /inquiries,/contact,/proxy-image,/stats,/export/products.csv and POST/PUT/DELETE across products, settings, upload, ai/*, watermark/*, admin/instagram, PATCH inquiries); (2) admin seeded session (owner@samratglassemporium.com) → 200 on /stats and /auth/me (email returned), 200 on /contact; (3) non-admin visitor@example.com session → 403 on every admin route; (4) CSRF guard → 403 when X-Requested-With header missing on POST/PUT/DELETE; (5) SSRF proxy correctly rejects http scheme, localhost, 127.0.0.1, 169.254.169.254, 10.0.0.1, and example.com with 400 (no 500, no content leaked); allowed unsplash URL returns 400 (not 500) — safe generic error; (6) public reads GET /products, /products/categories, /settings work anonymously (200), /reviews returns 422 without product_id which is a valid Pydantic query-param requirement, not an auth issue; (7) public writes POST /contact and POST /catalogue-request succeed with X-Requested-With header; /inquiries burst hits 429 well within budget. Frontend: /admin renders the AdminAuthGate with data-testid=admin-signin-btn when anonymous, and after seeding session_token cookie shows 'SIGNED IN AS owner@samratglassemporium.com' with data-testid=admin-email + admin-signout-btn and full Admin Dashboard tabs.

minor.issue: Returns 500 for authenticated admin sessions — pre-existing data bug (some inquiry rows have empty customer_email failing Pydantic validation). Explicitly flagged by review request as OUT OF SCOPE for this security task. Recommend adding Optional[EmailStr] or migrating legacy rows.

action_items: Optional (out-of-scope of security task): fix pre-existing GET /api/inquiries 500 error by relaxing customer_email validation to Optional[EmailStr] or by data-migrating legacy rows lacking a valid email.
critical_code_review_comments: Auth/authz surface is tight: 401 for anonymous, 403 for non-admin, 403 for authenticated writes missing X-Requested-With — verified across 20+ routes.

SSRF proxy correctly blocks non-HTTPS, loopback (localhost/127.0.0.1), link-local metadata (169.254.169.254), RFC1918 (10.0.0.1) and non-allowlisted hosts (example.com). All safe 400 responses, no 500, no content leaked.
Rate limits confirmed active on /inquiries (per-IP burst → 429 within a handful of requests).
AdminAuthGate correctly renders 3 states: checking → anonymous card → authenticated dashboard. data-testid attributes present on admin-signin-btn, admin-email, admin-signout-btn.
Legacy data pitfall: GET /api/inquiries 500 for admin — noted by review request as pre-existing; would benefit from making customer_email Optional in the Pydantic response model.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (both anon and authed AdminAuthGate states verified)

test_credentials: Used seeded session tokens against test_database Mongo: admin=owner@samratglassemporium.com (from ADMIN_EMAILS env) and visitor=visitor@example.com. Cleanup performed after test — 8 sessions and 8 users removed.
seed_data_creation: Temporary admin + visitor sessions inserted into user_sessions/users collections (test_database); all cleaned up post-test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Security lockdown (401/403 gating, CSRF guard, SSRF proxy, rate limits, AdminAuthGate) is fully working. If retesting, note that DB_NAME in /app/backend/.env is `test_database` (not `samrat_glass_emporium` as the credentials doc suggests) — seed sessions into `test_database.user_sessions`. The only known 500 is GET /api/inquiries for admin (legacy data bug, not part of this task).
rca of the issue: No security defect found in this iteration. Only known unrelated bug is GET /api/inquiries 500 — RCA: legacy inquiry documents have empty/invalid customer_email that fail the Pydantic Inquiry response model's EmailStr validation. Mitigation: change customer_email to Optional[EmailStr] or migrate legacy rows to add a valid placeholder. Explicitly flagged as out of scope by the review request.
